verbGlossary.SCREENS: {source} evaluated through a prequalification process for financial capacity, safety record, and trade competency before being eligible to bid
verbGlossary.ADMINISTERED BY: {source} responsible for managing and enforcing {target} instrument, process, or general conditions on the project
verbGlossary.MANDATED BY: Regulatory or public {target} that requires a procurement step, certification, or compliance document by law or policy
verbGlossary.ASSEMBLED BY: {source} that compiles bid packages, closeout documents, or procurement deliverables from multiple sources
verbGlossary.ISSUED TO: {source} that receives a bid package, RFP, or procurement solicitation for response
verbGlossary.SCOPED BY: {source}defines the trade scope of work included in a bid package
verbGlossary.RESPONDED TO BY: {source} that submits a qualification statement or proposal in response to a solicitation
verbGlossary.ISSUED BY: {source} that formally releases a procurement document, contract instrument, or award notice
verbGlossary.SUBMITTED BY: {source} that formally delivers a bid, proposal, or compliance certification
verbGlossary.PERFORMED BY: {source} that carries out the bid evaluation or leveling analysis
verbGlossary.CONSULTED ON BY: {source}whose technical input is sought during bid evaluation or scope clarification
verbGlossary.REVIEWED BY: {source} that examines {target}, closeout package, or evaluation for accuracy and completeness
verbGlossary.AWARDED TO: {source} selected to receive {target}, purchase order, or notice to proceed
verbGlossary.AUTHORIZED BY: {source}that grants formal approval for {target} award or expenditure
verbGlossary.EXECUTED BY: Owner {target} that signs and executes the prime contract with the construction team
verbGlossary.CONTRACTED UNDER: {source} that performs work governed by a specific contract instrument
verbGlossary.SELF-PERFORMED UNDER: {target} division that executes scope of work with its own forces rather than subcontracting
verbGlossary.NEGOTIATED BY: {source} that negotiates the commercial terms of a master service agreement or contract
verbGlossary.DEFINED BY: Design professional who authors the technical scope, specifications, or requirements in contract documents
verbGlossary.BIDS ON: {target} that submits competitive pricing against a defined scope of work
verbGlossary.SELF-PERFORMS: {target} division that executes a scope of work with its own craft labor instead of subcontracting
verbGlossary.STIPULATED BY: Owner or public {target} that establishes binding contract terms in general conditions
verbGlossary.BOUND BY: {source} contractually obligated to comply with general conditions or flow-down terms
verbGlossary.WITHHOLDS PER: {target} that deducts retainage from subcontractor payments per contract retainage terms
verbGlossary.WITHHELD FROM PER: {target} whose progress payments are reduced by retainage per contract terms
verbGlossary.ASSESSED AGAINST: {source} against which liquidated damages are charged for schedule or performance failure
verbGlossary.FLOWED DOWN TO: {target} to whom prime contract liquidated damages provisions are passed via subcontract
verbGlossary.ENFORCED BY: {target} that exercises contractual rights to collect damages or enforce warranty obligations
verbGlossary.ESTABLISHED BY: {source}that sets allowance dollar amounts in {target} for undefined or provisional scope items
verbGlossary.SPECIFIED BY: {source}that defines allowance scope or material standards in contract documents
verbGlossary.UNDERWRITTEN BY: Surety or insurance {target} that assumes financial risk by issuing a bond or insurance policy
verbGlossary.FURNISHED BY: Contractor or {target} that procures and delivers a required bond or insurance certificate to the obligee
verbGlossary.REQUIRED BY: Owner, lender, or public {target} that mandates bonds or insurance as a condition of contract
verbGlossary.BONDED UNDER: {source}whose contractual performance and payment obligations are secured by a surety bond
verbGlossary.PROTECTED BY: {target} or supplier whose right to payment is secured by a payment bond
verbGlossary.COLLECTED BY: {target} that gathers insurance certificates from all subcontractors and vendors for compliance tracking
verbGlossary.PROCURED BY: {source} that purchases builders risk or project-wide insurance coverage
verbGlossary.PROVIDED BY: {target}, fabricator, or supplier that furnishes a warranty for installed work or materials
verbGlossary.CERTIFIED BY: {target} or authority that formally certifies a completion milestone or compliance document
verbGlossary.ACCEPTED BY: {target} that formally accepts a completion milestone or closeout deliverable
verbGlossary.ACHIEVED BY: {target} that reaches substantial or final completion milestone per contract requirements
verbGlossary.INSPECTED BY: Regulatory {target} that performs final inspection before certificate of occupancy or completion
verbGlossary.CLOSED OUT BY: {target} that completes all punch list, warranty, and documentation requirements to close their contract
verbGlossary.COMPLIED WITH BY: {source} that meets prevailing wage, safety, or regulatory requirements specified in contract
verbGlossary.CERTIFIED UNDER: {source} that holds a diversity, DBE, or minority business certification recognized by {target}
verbGlossary.TRACKED BY: {source} that monitors a procurement metric, compliance rate, or retention statistic over time
verbGlossary.MEASURED BY: {source} that calculates and reports a procurement performance metric
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKED BY: {source}that compares procurement cycle times or metrics across projects in a portfolio
verbGlossary.ATTRIBUTED TO: {source} whose actions or trade scope are the source of a change order or retention metric
